title: Nutty Velvety Irish Olive Stir-fry
description: A crowd-pleasing Irish dish featuring blanched olives with a creamy nutty sauce, perfect for lunch or dinner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Blanch olives in boiling water for about 3 minutes, then rinse with cold water and pat dry.
Toss broccoli florets, carrots, and celery stalks with olive oil, nutritional yeast, soy sauce, and half a cup of water. Spread them evenly on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Roast in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes until vegetables are tender and slightly caramelized. Remove from oven and set aside.
In a blender or food processor, combine remaining soy sauce, nutritional yeast, water, blended olives, onion, basil, and olive oil until smooth. Season with salt to taste.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add roasted vegetables and stir-fry for about 5 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken slightly as it cooks.
Pour the creamy sauce into the pan with vegetables, stirring constantly until all ingredients are well combined and heated through. Adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve hot as a side dish or over rice or quinoa.
